raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Formal Complaint Regarding Violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) - Billing Error ( Unapplied Credit for Returned Merchandise ) Citi Case ID XXXX Background : I am filing this complaint against Citi for its failure to resolve a clear billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), 15 U.S.C. 1666. On XX/XX/year>, I returned merchandise to the merchant. 

Documentary Evidence of Return : Proof of Delivery : XXXX Tracking XXXX confirms the merchant received the returned goods on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Merchant Admission : In a recorded chat on XX/XX/year>, the merchant representative ( XXXX XXXX XXXX explicitly confirmed receipt of the goods and stated, We processed the refund to your XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Nature of the Dispute : Despite this overwhelming evidence, Citi has closed the dispute in the merchants favor based on a demonstrably false claim that the merchandise is still in my possession. This is factually impossible as I have provided the bank with the XXXX Proof of Delivery and the merchant 's own confirmation of receipt. 

Regulatory Violation : Under the FCBA, a creditor must conduct a reasonable investigation into a billing error. Citi has failed to meet this standard by ignoring the physical proof of return and the merchants own admission of a processed refund. The merchant has failed to execute the transaction they confirmed, resulting in a missing credit on my statement.
